author: lukaszrozmej
content:
1. So you are saying we should also develop the host program to call the zkvm?
2. IMO The host program and the format should be standarized, so guest programs are interoperable
3. All this sounds so much rust biased, that making it an even playing field and proper standards, while aiming for prod this year sound ambitious

author: 0xrubo
content:
> i believe that host app is a third party that fetches the data from exec client, serializes it and feeds the guest app, right?
<@628018527470616606> by *third party* above, i meant that the host app is not on us (we don't develop it) -- someone who wants to prove blocks with the guest and zkvm of their choice. right <@427491045308235776>?

author: kevaundray
content:
1) no, you essentially just need to pick a serialization library for serializing and deserializing the input. The input itself will be defined in execution specs.

2) I think how witness data is fetched won't need to be standardized, someone can use eth_getProofs or debug_executionWitness or infura -- as long as the input format matches then it doesn't matter

3) We started doing this a year ago, so a lot of it has already been made less rust based, like the RV64IM standard instead of using rust forks and the IO standards. I think its very achievable and of course, I'm happy to help wherever I can to accelerate things on the nethermind side

author: kevaundray
content: The zkVM itself just needs an `input.bin` file and the Nethermind ELF
